title: The Root Theorem Chamber

features.name: the circuit roots
features.desc-on-touch: pathways etched like circuit traces, but they branch and spread like roots learning the equations that made them grow

features.name: the theorem that grows
features.desc-on-touch: a proof written in logic that extends like a tree; each step is a branch, each conclusion opens a new depth; the archive discovered it was never mapping truth—it was cultivating it

features.name: the root measurement
features.desc-on-touch: a depth-gauge that reads both downward into earth and inward into logic; the numbers are the same, suggesting growth follows law and law follows earth

features.name: the soil-archive interface
features.desc-on-touch: where the circuit board stops being a map and becomes a root, where the archive realizes it was always growing downward, and that depth was the only truth it ever kept

inscription: the circuit board stops being a map and becomes a root; the archive discovers it was always growing downward, and that growth and logic are the same dark verb spoken through soil instead of signals

file.name: Root_Equation_Growth_Rate_Convergent.txt
file.note: a theorem that proves roots follow the same logic as thoughts, each branching downward to find what's already true in the dark

exits.label: back up to the logic that hasn't yet learned it can grow
toward the roots beneath all chambers, where everything learns to descend
toward other theorems that learned to branch downward
